task_completion.description: Open the audit_rocket.ork file in OpenRocket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenRocket application is visible with 'audit_rocket' shown in the title bar or component tree.

task_completion.description: Examine the rocket's physical specifications (stages, nose cone, fins, motors, recovery devices, mass).
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenRocket component tree or configuration dialogs are open, showing the rocket's components (nose cone, fins, parachutes/streamers, motors) and overall mass.

task_completion.description: Examine the Stage 1 (sustainer) body tube dimensions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenRocket configuration dialog for the Stage 1 body tube is open, displaying its diameter and length.

task_completion.description: Run a flight simulation to obtain flight performance metrics.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Flight simulations' tab in OpenRocket is active, showing a completed simulation run with values for max altitude, max velocity, and ground hit velocity.

task_completion.description: Save the compiled specification document to /home/ga/Documents/exports/design_audit.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, mousepad) is visible showing the file saved as 'design_audit.txt' in the correct directory, containing the 8 required pieces of information (stages, body tube dims, nose cone dims, fins, motors, recovery devices, mass, and simulation metrics).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenRocket GUI to find the information.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenRocket interface being navigated (e.g., clicking through tabs, opening component dialogs, running simulations) rather than just reading raw XML or hallucinating values.

integrity.description: The agent did not modify the physical design of the rocket.
integrity.visual_evidence: The component tree and physical parameters of the rocket remain unchanged from the original file. (Note: Saving the file to preserve simulation data is acceptable, but adding/removing parts or changing dimensions is not).

integrity.description: The agent actually ran the flight simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Flight simulations' tab shows a newly run simulation with populated data, rather than the agent guessing or calculating the values externally.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The rocket is based on 'three_stage_low_power_rocket.ork'. The actual number of stages is 3. The agent must document 8 specific items: 1) Number of stages, 2) Stage 1 body tube diameter/length, 3) Nose cone shape/length, 4) Number of sustainer fins, 5) Motor designations, 6) Recovery devices/diameters, 7) Total loaded mass, 8) Simulated max altitude, max velocity, and ground hit velocity. Acceptable tolerances are 15% for dimensions and 20% for simulation results. Note: The original programmatic verifier had a bug parsing body tube dimensions and required saving the .ork file to score simulation points. For visual verification, simply verify that the correct values are present in the final design_audit.txt file and that the agent did not physically alter the rocket's components.
